cycle_stage.rationale: ISM PMI at 53.3 shows expansion, with neither the yield curve nor unemployment flashing late-cycle strain.

narrative: Regime read: Cautiously Neutral (composite score -0.04 on a -1 to +1 scale). Finance conditions are roughly balanced (-0.05), economic data is roughly balanced (+0.09), and market psychology leans fearful (-0.21), with the economy in an Expansion phase.

Finance: the yield curve is positively sloped (+0.39pp), the S&P 500 trades at 28.9x earnings against a ~16.5x historical average, and 4 of 5 tracked sectors are positive over the past month — the classical efficient-market / factor-based baseline this dashboard's behavioral overlay adjusts. Economics: ISM PMI is at 53.3 (expansion), CPI is running 3.5% YoY against a 3.63% Fed funds rate, and unemployment sits at 4.2%. ISM PMI at 53.3 shows expansion, with neither the yield curve nor unemployment flashing late-cycle strain. (standard business-cycle framing — sentiment reads differently depending on where the cycle stands.)

Fundamentals (finance + economics) alone would put the read at +0.01; current psychology shifts that to -0.04 — a 0.05-point gap that makes positioning more cautious than fundamentals alone would justify. Psychology is weighted 23% of the final read right now (up from a 15% calm-market floor) because of how extreme it currently is. Per the Adaptive Markets Hypothesis this dashboard is built on, that gap — not any single pillar — is the actual signal.

No single extreme-positioning signal (herding, loss-aversion-driven hedging, overconfidence, or a Minsky-style extended-calm streak) is flashing right now — sentiment, hedging demand, and volatility all look within a normal range.

allocation_tilts.disclaimer: Illustrative starting points generated from the scores above via simple, documented rules — not personalized investment advice. Apply your own judgment, mandate constraints, and risk tolerance.

strategies.name: Broad equity risk-on / momentum continuation
strategies.rationale: Composite (-0.04), breadth, or psychology (-0.21) isn't clearly confirming a Risk-On trend worth chasing right now.

strategies.name: Quality / defensive rotation
strategies.rationale: Composite is cautious (-0.04) and/or valuations are stretched with fear-leaning psychology (-0.21) — a case for rotating toward quality and defensives.

strategies.name: Barbell: quality equities + duration
strategies.rationale: Either the regime isn't balanced enough or real yields aren't attractive enough to justify a barbell right now.

strategies.name: Inflation hedge / real-assets tilt
strategies.rationale: Gold is already flagged Overweight above (fear-leaning psychology and/or hot CPI) — the same signal supports a broader real-assets hedge.

strategies.name: Valuation-aware trim / raise cash on strength
strategies.rationale: Valuations are significantly stretched (valuation component -1.00) even though the broader regime isn't bearish (-0.04) — a case for trimming winners into strength rather than adding new risk at these prices.

strategies.name: Stay neutral on net exposure
strategies.rationale: The composite regime is genuinely balanced (-0.04) — no pillar is dominant enough to justify a strong directional tilt on overall market exposure.
